description: Letter from Mevorakh b. Yiṣḥaq from Alexandria to his father in law, Seror (Surur) b. Ḥayyim b. Sabra in Fustat. Written a month after another letter: CUL Or.1080 J264 (Doc. #100 in Frenkel). He mentions pillaging in Maḥalla (the writer hopes that Abū l-Ḵayr Mubārak is safe), an epidemic (dever) in Fusṭāṭ and that the writer himself had been robbed. Also mentions Ibn Naḥum the cantor. Probably written in the 1060s. The scribe gives his name too: Musa b. Da'ud b. Ezra. Information from CUDL. ASE.
tags: epidemic, Illness, illness letter 969-1517, epidemic: dever
description_he: מכתב ממבורך בן יצחק מאלכסנדריה לחמיו סרור בן חיים בפוסטאט, על שוד, מגפה ובזז המחלה, כ-1060
